story.title: View Payment History
story.narrative: As an authenticated policyholder, I want to review my payment history so that I can confirm what I paid, when it was processed, and which payment method was used without contacting support.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/payments/history returns only payment records belonging to the authenticated user identified from the Authentication object.
The Payment History page is accessible from the Bills navigation section for authenticated users.
The page displays payment records in descending processedDate order.
The page displays a minimum of 12 months of payment history and supports retrieval of all available historical records.
Each payment row displays processedDate, transactionReference, description, paymentMethodReference, amount, and status.
paymentMethodReference displays only a masked identifier in the format '•••• 4242' or equivalent last-four format.
The API never returns full PAN, CVV, PIN, magnetic stripe data, or any Sensitive Authentication Data.
Users attempting to access payment records that do not belong to them receive HTTP 403 Forbidden and no payment data is returned.
When no payment records exist, the UI displays the exact copy string 'No payment history available.'
The transaction table supports pagination with a default page size of 25 records.
Supported payment statuses are PROCESSED, PENDING, FAILED, REFUNDED, and REVERSED.
Autopay transactions, one-time payments, refunds, reversals, and billing adjustments are included in payment history.
Every successful payment-history retrieval generates an audit log entry containing userId, eventType='PAYMENT_HISTORY_VIEW', timestamp, and outcome='SUCCESS'.
Every failed or unauthorized payment-history retrieval generates an audit log entry containing userId when available, eventType='PAYMENT_HISTORY_VIEW', timestamp, and outcome='FAILURE'.
Unexpected server errors return HTTP 500 with the exact message 'Unable to retrieve payment history.' and do not expose payment data or internal system details.

resolved_decisions.history_retention_display: Display all available historical payment records with a minimum requirement of 12 months available.
resolved_decisions.pagination: Enabled with default page size of 25 records.
resolved_decisions.included_transaction_types: Autopay, one-time payments, refunds, reversals, and billing adjustments.
resolved_decisions.shared_access: Users may view only their own payment history.

screens.description: Primary payment history page — loaded state with transaction table.
Admin role view of the payments area.
Receipt detail view — explicitly out of scope for this story, included for design context only.

compliance_requirements_applied.how_addressed: No Sensitive Authentication Data is stored, returned, logged, or displayed by the feature.
Payment methods are displayed only as masked references using last-four format.
Users can retrieve only their own payment records based on authenticated identity.
Endpoint access requires explicit authorization configuration and denies access by default.
Payment-history access events are logged for monitoring purposes.
Audit records capture user identity, event type, timestamp, and success or failure outcome.
Audit logs remain protected and accessible only to authorized personnel.
User-scoped queries, parameterized data access, and API-layer authorization enforcement are required.
